description: Compile-time refinement violation on a statically-known STRING literal against a named shape (§53.4.2 rule 2 / §53.11 E-CONTRACT-001). The cell type string(email) is initialized with the literal "not-an-email", which demonstrably fails the email structural predicate. Per §53.4.2 rule 2 a built-in named shape against a string literal is statically decidable (T-PRED-1), so the compiler proves the violation at COMPILE time and fires E-CONTRACT-001 — the string-shape sibling of refinement-literal-violation-pos (numeric) and the GAP-2 coverage the ss62 verify-pass flagged (existing cases only used numeric).
rationale: §53.4.2 rule 2 cites `let e: string(email) = "user@example.com"` as statically provable; §53.11 E-CONTRACT-001 fires when the compiler can prove a literal violates the predicate. "not-an-email" fails the email shape (no @/domain), so the violation is proven statically.
